title: 10 best herbal decoctions for motion sickness
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for motion sickness are natural remedies made by steeping herbs in hot water to create a soothing, gentle brew. These decoctions help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness, nausea, and vomiting by calming the stomach and reducing inflammation. Examples include peppermint, ginger, and lemon balm decoctions, which can be consumed before or during travel to alleviate discomfort. By using herbal decoctions, individuals with motion sickness can enjoy activities like boating, flying, or road trips without worrying about debilitating symptoms, greatly improving their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with motion sickness because it has a natural calming effect on the stomach, reducing nausea and vomiting symptoms. The menthol content in peppermint also has anti-inflammatory properties that help to ease digestive discomfort caused by motion. Additionally, peppermint's soothing aroma can help to calm the mind and body, further alleviating feelings of queasiness. As a result, herbal peppermint decoctions have been shown to be an effective natural remedy for alleviating the symptoms of motion sickness, providing relief from discomfort during travel or other activities that trigger nausea.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint decoction helps relax muscles and alleviate spasms in the stomach, which can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms like nausea and vomiting.
Digestive aid properties: Peppermint's ability to stimulate digestion and relieve indigestion can help alleviate discomfort and nausea associated with motion sickness.
Vasoconstrictor properties: Peppermint's vasoconstricting effects can help constrict blood vessels in the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort that can worsen motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Menthofuran: Menthofuran is a monoterpene ketone that contributes to the anti-emetic properties of peppermint, helping alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Menthol: Menthol is a monoterpene alcohol that has a cooling effect on the digestive system, which may help reduce nausea and stomach discomfort caused by motion sickness.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a polyphenolic compound that exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the severity of motion sickness symptoms by protecting the body from oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of menthol, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and digestive properties that can help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes contain menthol and other essential oils that can help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea associated with motion sickness.
Stems: The stems also contain menthol and other compounds that can help to ease digestive discomfort and reduce the symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their highest oil content.
Cut the harvested mentha x piperita into small pieces and weigh out 20-30 grams for a standard decoction.
Combine the cut mentha x piperita with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to extract.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with motion sickness because its natural compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, have potent anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. When consumed before or during travel, these compounds work to calm the stomach and alleviate nausea, dizziness, and vomiting caused by motion sickness. Additionally, ginger's natural sedative effects help regulate blood flow and reduce feelings of queasiness, providing quick relief from discomfort and promoting a smoother journey.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant property: Ginger's ability to stimulate digestion helps to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by promoting gastric motility and reducing stomach contractions.

Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can exacerbate symptoms of motion sickness, such as stomach upset and discomfort.

Vasoactive property: Ginger's vasoactive compounds help to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow, which may help to reduce feelings of dizziness and lightheadedness associated with motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These iridoid compounds help with motion sickness by inhibiting the serotonin receptors in the brain, which are responsible for nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Shogaols: These ginger-derived compounds help with motion sickness by inhibiting the prostaglandins in the stomach, which contribute to nausea and vomiting, and also by stimulating the digestive system to improve gut motility.

Zingibain: This proteolytic enzyme helps with motion sickness by breaking down the proteins in the digestive system that can contribute to nausea and vomiting, and also by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation in the gut.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of ginger are also used to make decoctions for motion sickness, as they contain similar compounds to the rhizome and are often used interchangeably.
Leaves: Although less common, the leaves of ginger are occasionally used to make decoctions for motion sickness, possibly due to their reported anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100 grams of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes for decoction preparation.
Wash the harvested rhizomes under cold running water for 5 minutes to remove dirt.
Peel the washed rhizomes using a vegetable peeler to expose their inner flesh.
Chop the peeled rhizomes into small pieces weighing about 20-30 grams per serving.
Simmer the chopped rhizomes in 1 liter of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create decoction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with motion sickness because it has a calming effect on the body's natural response to motion stimuli. The sweet, floral scent of lavender helps to reduce feelings of anxiety and unease that can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms. Additionally, the soothing properties of lavender have been shown to ease digestive discomfort and relieve nausea associated with motion sickness, making it a popular remedy for those prone to sea or air travel sickness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: English lavender helps to relax the muscles and calm spasms in the stomach, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) property: Lavender's calming effect on the nervous system can help reduce feelings of anxiety and stress that often accompany motion sickness, making it easier to cope with the discomfort.

Gastroprotective property: English lavender has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects on the digestive tract, which may help soothe and protect the stomach lining from irritation caused by motion sickness, reducing symptoms like nausea and vomiting.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with motion sickness by reducing anxiety and stress responses, which can exacerbate nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has sedative and anti-anxiety effects, which can help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness, such as nausea, vomiting, and dizziness.
Apigenin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate the stomach cramps and nausea associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their calming and sedative properties, which help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Flowers: They are used due to their calming and anti-anxiety effects, which can help reduce the severity of motion sickness.
Stems: They are used due to their mild sedative properties, which can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom time and dry them in a warm area for 2 weeks.
Crush 2 tablespoons of dried flowers into fine powder using a mortar and pestle to release essential oils.
Combine the powdered flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant container to create a decoction.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with motion sickness because of its calming and soothing properties. The apigenin and luteolin flavonoids present in chamomile interact with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. This tranquilizing effect helps alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, further alleviating symptoms. By sipping on a warm herbal decoction of chamomile tea, individuals may find relief from the discomforts of motion sickness, allowing for a more enjoyable travel experience.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-anxiety effects: Chamomile's sedative properties can help calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress associated with motion sickness.
Antispasmodic effects: Chamomile's antispasmodic properties can help relax the stomach muscles, reducing nausea and vomiting that often accompany motion sickness.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to motion sickness symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that acts as a natural antihistamine, which helps to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness, such as nausea, dizziness, and vomiting, by blocking the action of histamine on the brain.
Linalool: A terpene that has a sedative effect, helping to reduce anxiety and stress associated with motion sickness, which can exacerbate symptoms.

Apigenin-7-O-glucoside: A glycoside form of apigenin that is more easily absorbed by the body, which also has antihistamine and antispasmodic properties, helping to alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and calming properties, which help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Seeds: Contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that may help reduce nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.
Leaves: May be used to make decoctions that help calm the digestive system and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried flowers in a clean and dry environment to ensure quality.
Grind 2-4 grams of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 200 milliliters of boiling water with 2-4 grams of the ground flowers in a heat-resistant glass cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes and then strain it using a piece of cheesecloth.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew decoctions helps with motion sickness because of its ability to relax the muscles, calm the stomach, and reduce inflammation in the body. The natural compounds present in feverfew, such as parthenolide, have been shown to exhibit anti-nausea properties that help alleviate symptoms associated with motion sickness. By soothing the digestive system and reducing feelings of queasiness, feverfew decoctions can provide quick relief from nausea, dizziness, and headaches commonly experienced during travel or other activities that cause motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide: Feverfew's parthenolide content has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Chamazulene: Feverfew's chamazulene content has been shown to have a positive effect on the digestive system, which can help reduce symptoms of motion sickness such as stomach upset and queasiness.

Essential oils: The essential oils present in feverfew decoctions, including camphor and borneol, have natural anti-nausea and anti-anxiety properties that can help soothe the stomach and reduce feelings of unease associated with motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Cnicin: This sesquiterpene lactone has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate the nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Parthenolide: This sesquiterpene lactone has been found to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce the severity of motion sickness symptoms, such as dizziness and nausea.

Tanacetol (also known as Camphor): As a GABA receptor antagonist, tanacetol can help stabilize the balance and equilibrium systems, which can help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness and vertigo.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.
Roots: They are used because of their ability to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which can contribute to motion sickness.
Flowers: They are used for their calming effects and to help regulate digestive issues, which can be triggered by motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers from a trusted source for medicinal use.
Combine the dried flowers with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with motion sickness because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce nausea and vomiting. The bitter compounds in dandelion also stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, which are often triggered by motion sickness. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic properties can help to eliminate excess fluids and electrolytes that can contribute to feelings of nausea and dizziness. By addressing these underlying physiological imbalances, dandelion decoctions may provide relief from the distressing symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Stomachic property: Dandelion's stomachic property helps to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in dandelion decoctions help to neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to symptoms of motion sickness.

Choleretic property: Dandelion's choleretic property helps to stimulate bile production and improve liver function, which can aid in digestion and relieve nausea and discomfort associated with motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Luteolin: This flavonoid may help alleviate motion sickness by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, reducing the severity of nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Taraxasterol: This triterpene saponin could contribute to the anti-emetic (vomiting-preventing) effects of dandelion decoctions by modulating the gut-brain axis and reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.
Iridoid glycosides (e.g., taraxasterol glycosides): These iridoid glycosides may exhibit anti-emetic properties by interacting with opioid receptors in the gut, thereby reducing nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain taraxasterol, a compound that may help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids, which may contribute to their potential anti-nausea properties.
Flowers: They contain sesquiterpene lactones, which may have a beneficial effect on motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and leaves from a clean and organic environment in late spring or early summer.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in a shaded area for 1 to 2 weeks.
Combine 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried taraxacum officinale roots and 2 to 3 teaspoons of dried taraxacum officinale leaves.
Boil 1 liter of water and steep the dried taraxacum officinale mixture for 5 to 10 minutes in a clean tea infuser.
Strain the taraxacum officinale decoction and let it cool for 30 minutes before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with motion sickness because it contains compounds that have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with travel. The herbal infusion also helps to soothe the stomach and alleviate symptoms of vertigo and dizziness caused by motion. Additionally, lemon balm's natural sedative properties can help to reduce anxiety and stress, further contributing to its effectiveness in alleviating the discomforts of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Anticholinergic activity: Lemon balm's anticholinergic compounds help to reduce the symptoms of motion sickness by blocking the action of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that can exacerbate nausea and vomiting.
Anxiolytic effects: The calming properties of lemon balm have been shown to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which are common precursors to motion sickness.

Gastrointestinal soothing compounds: Lemon balm's flavonoids and terpenes possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea, vomiting, and abdominal cramps associated with motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are thought to contribute to motion sickness symptoms.
Limonene: A terpene found in lemon balm, limonene has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Glycosides (including melitoside): These compounds have been found to have a sedative effect on the central nervous system, which can help reduce the anxiety and stress that often exacerbate motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and calming properties that help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers contain similar essential oils as the leaves, making them effective in soothing nausea and reducing motion sickness symptoms.

Roots: The roots of Melissa officinalis are also used to make decoctions for motion sickness, as they contain a higher concentration of essential oils compared to the leaves and flowers, which can provide a more potent remedy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure freshness.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7 days or by machine for 2 hours.
Chop 2-3 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves into small pieces to increase surface area.
Steep the chopped leaves in 150 milliliters of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids to create a clear herbal infusion ready for consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop decoctions helps with motion sickness because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids that work together to alleviate symptoms. The decoction's calming properties help to reduce feelings of nausea and dizziness, while its anti-inflammatory compounds may soothe stomach muscles and ease queasiness. Additionally, maypop's natural antihistamines can counteract the release of histamine in response to motion stimuli, further reducing discomfort and promoting a sense of relaxation.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Maypop contains flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce symptoms of motion sickness by calming digestive upset and reducing inflammation in the stomach.

Alkaloids: The alkaloid compounds present in maypop decoctions, such as quinolizidine and pyrrolizidine, have been found to exhibit a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness like dizziness and nausea.

Tannins: Maypop's tannins have been shown to have antispasmodic properties, helping to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, which can reduce the frequency and severity of vomiting and other symptoms associated with motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Isovitexin: Isovitexin, a flavonoid glycoside, helps alleviate motion sickness by exerting anticholinesterase activity, which counteracts the effects of acetylcholine that can exacerbate nausea and vomiting.

Hyperoside: Hyperoside, a flavonoid glycoside, is thought to help with motion sickness by inhibiting the release of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and acetylcholine, which play a role in the development of nausea and vomiting.

Flavonoids (including vitexin and isovitexin): Flavonoids, such as vitexin and isovitexin, help alleviate motion sickness by reducing anxiety and stress through their antioxidant properties and their ability to modulate the central nervous system.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Passiflora incarnata are commonly used to make decoctions for motion sickness because they contain the highest concentration of passionflower's medicinal compounds.
Leaves: The leaves of Passiflora incarnata are used in decoctions for motion sickness due to their ability to soothe the nervous system and reduce anxiety.

Fruits: The fruits of Passiflora incarnata, specifically the juice extracted from them, is used to make decoctions for motion sickness because it contains a higher concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds compared to other parts of the plant.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 grams of fresh or dried plant material early in the morning.
Rinse the plant material with water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine the plant material with 500 milliliters of water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid reduces slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with motion sickness because it contains flavonoids, a type of antioxidant that has been shown to stabilize blood vessels and reduce inflammation. This can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach discomfort. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe the digestive system, reducing queasiness and vomiting associated with motion sickness. Additionally, its calming effects can help to reduce anxiety and stress related to travel, making it a natural remedy for those prone to motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help protect against free radical damage caused by prolonged exposure to motion, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with motion sickness.

Vasodilation properties: The decoction's ability to dilate blood vessels may help increase blood flow to the brain, improving cognitive function and reducing symptoms of disorientation and dizziness commonly experienced during motion sickness.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, a common cause of motion sickness, thereby alleviating symptoms such as vertigo, nausea, and vomiting.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help alleviate motion sickness by improving blood flow to the inner ear and brain, reducing the severity of symptoms like dizziness and nausea.

Bilobalide: This triterpene derivative may help reduce the incidence of motion sickness by inhibiting the release of neurotransmitters involved in nausea and vomiting, such as serotonin and acetylcholine.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may contribute to its ability to alleviate motion sickness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in flavonoids, terpenoids, and other compounds that help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Seeds: The seeds are used to create a decoction that may help alleviate dizziness and nausea associated with motion sickness.
Buds: The buds are used in some traditional Chinese medicine preparations to help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 ginkgo biloba leaves from a mature tree and clean them thoroughly with water.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days or less.
Measure 2-3 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves per 250 milliliters of water for decoction.
Combine the measured leaves with water in a saucepan and bring to a boil over medium heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 30-60 minutes then strain the decoction through a cheesecloth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian decoctions helps with motion sickness because its unique blend of active compounds, particularly valepotriates and isovaleric acid, work together to calm the digestive system and alleviate nausea. The sedative properties of valerian root also help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can be disrupted by the sensory conflicts associated with motion sickness. By soothing the stomach and calming the mind, herbal valerian decoctions provide a natural remedy for alleviating the uncomfortable symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian root has been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm nausea and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.

Antispasmodic properties: Valerian's antispasmodic properties can help to relax muscle contractions in the stomach and intestines, reducing feelings of discomfort and nausea associated with motion sickness.

Gastroprotective properties: Valerian decoctions may also possess gastroprotective effects, which can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This terpenoid compound helps alleviate motion sickness by interacting with the GABA receptors in the brain, which can help reduce anxiety and nausea.
Valeranone: As a valerenic acid derivative, valeranone has been shown to have sedative properties, which can help reduce the symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness and nausea.
Isovaleric acid: This organic acid has been found to have a sedative effect and may help reduce the severity of motion sickness symptoms by acting on the central nervous system.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are rich in valerenic acid, a compound that has been shown to have sedative and anti-anxiety properties, helping to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness.
Leaves: The leaves of Valeriana officinalis contain valerenic acid and other compounds that contribute to its calming effects, making it a useful ingredient in decoctions to treat motion sickness.
Flowers: The flowers of Valeriana officinalis have a soothing effect, which helps to reduce anxiety and alleviate the nausea associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of valeriana officinalis in the spring or fall for optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine 5 to 10 grams with 1 liter of water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound decoctions helps with motion sickness because it contains a compound called marrubium, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a decoction, horehound soothes the digestive system and reduces nausea, making it an effective remedy for alleviating motion sickness symptoms. Additionally, the herb's expectorant properties help to relieve congestion and discomfort associated with motion sickness, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable and relaxed during travel or other activities that cause motion-related distress.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Horehound's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the stomach and digestive tract, reducing the frequency and severity of nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Expectorant properties: Horehound's expectorant properties can help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear out of the lungs and airways, which can be beneficial in alleviating symptoms of motion sickness such as congestion and phlegm buildup.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Horehound's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and stomach, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the horehound plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Barks: The barks of the horehound plant contain glycosides that help to relax the stomach muscles, reducing the symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh or dried marrubium vulgare leaves in sufficient quantity for desired dosage.
Measure out two teaspoons of dried marrubium vulgare leaves or four teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Combine the measured herbs with one cup of boiling water and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Allow the decoction to cool before drinking, taking 1/2 cup three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with motion sickness because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and terpenes. These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea, dizziness, and vomiting. The decoction's antispasmodic effects can also help to relax the stomach muscles, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by travel. Additionally, elder's calming and soothing properties can promote a sense of relaxation, further alleviating feelings of unease associated with motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Anticholinergic properties: Elder decoction contains compounds that can help reduce cholinergic activity, which is thought to contribute to the development of motion sickness symptoms such as nausea and vomiting.

Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in elder decoction may help dilate blood vessels, improving blood circulation and reducing symptoms of dizziness and lightheadedness associated with motion sickness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the stomach and digestive tract, alleviating nausea, vomiting, and other gastrointestinal symptoms caused by motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the elder plant contain iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Bark: The bark of the elder plant contains anthraquinones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe stomach upset caused by motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves.
Combine the herbs with 8 oz of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup or bottle.
Consume 1/2 to 1 cup of the prepared decoction 30 minutes before travel.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow decoctions helps with motion sickness because of its unique ability to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. The bitter compounds present in yarrow, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have a soothing effect on the digestive system, which helps to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness like dizziness and queasiness. Additionally, yarrow's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, further contributing to its effectiveness in relieving motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with motion sickness, making it a natural remedy to alleviate nausea and dizziness.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of yarrow decoction can help relax the digestive tract and relieve muscle spasms that often occur during motion sickness, reducing symptoms like vomiting and abdominal pain.

Vasoconstrictive: Yarrow's vasoconstrictive properties can help reduce blood flow to the stomach and intestines, which can alleviate nausea and prevent vomiting associated with motion sickness by reducing gastric acid production.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have anti-nausea and anti-motion sickness properties.
Buds: Young buds from the stem tips contain high amounts of flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the early morning or evening when they are at their peak potency.
Rinse the harvested plant material with cold filtered water to remove any dirt or debris and pat dry with a clean towel.
Combine 10-15 grams of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the hot water, then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Pour the cooled decoction into an amber-colored glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before consumption at a dosage of 1/4 cup 2-3 times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint decoctions helps with motion sickness because it contains a unique combination of compounds that have a calming effect on the digestive system. The menthol content in spearmint has natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as nausea and dizziness caused by motion sickness. Additionally, spearmint's soothing effects on the stomach muscles can also aid in reducing vomiting and discomfort associated with motion sickness. By promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing digestive turmoil, spearmint decoctions can provide effective relief from motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant: Spearmint decoction helps to stimulate digestive enzymes, which can ease nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. This is because it helps to regulate digestion and reduce inflammation in the gut.

Vaso-dilatory properties: The volatile oils present in spearmint decoction have been shown to dilate blood vessels, which can help to improve circulation and alleviate symptoms of dizziness and lightheadedness that often accompany motion sickness.

Antispasmodic properties: Spearmint decoction contains compounds that can help to relax smooth muscle spasms in the digestive tract, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting during motion sickness. By relaxing these muscles, spearmint may help to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Spearmint roots are often used to create a calming and soothing tea that can help alleviate nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.
Barks: The bark of the spearmint plant contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making it effective in treating symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh mentha spicata leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine the leaves with 250ml of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes, then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup.
Allow the decoction to cool slightly before taking one dose of 50ml three times daily as needed.
Store any remaining decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator and consume within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with motion sickness because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as an herbal remedy, licorice root can help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. The decoction's soothing effects on the stomach lining also calm digestive issues caused by travel-related stress. Additionally, licorice's natural antispasmodic properties relax the muscles in the stomach, alleviating cramps and discomfort that often accompany motion sickness. As a result, herbal licorice decoctions provide natural relief from the unpleasant symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract, thereby providing relief from motion sickness symptoms.

Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, which can help protect the body against oxidative stress caused by motion sickness, and may also have anti-inflammatory properties that contribute to their therapeutic effects.

Isoliquiritigenin: Exhibits anti-emetic properties, which means it can help to reduce vomiting and nausea associated with motion sickness, likely due to its ability to interact with neurotransmitters involved in nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and triterpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried licorice root extract into a clean glass jar.
Boil 8 ounces of water in a pot and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the measured licorice root extract to the boiling water and steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool before consuming 1/2 cup 30 minutes prior to traveling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap decoctions helps with motion sickness because it contains a compound called baicalin, which has natural anti-nausea properties. When consumed as a decoction, skullcap can help to reduce the symptoms of motion sickness by calming the stomach and alleviating feelings of dizziness and vertigo. Additionally, skullcap's ability to relax the nervous system can also help to reduce stress and anxiety associated with travel or other situations that trigger motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Scopoletin: Scopoletin, a sesquiterpene lactone found in skullcap, has been shown to possess antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the nausea, vomiting, and dizziness associated with motion sickness.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in skullcap decoctions have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce the severity of motion sickness by neutralizing free radicals and soothing the digestive system.

Essential oils: The essential oils extracted from skullcap leaves, such as borneol and terpinen-4-ol, have been found to possess antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties, which can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the skullcap plant are rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of skullcap contain anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gently boil one teaspoon of dried scutellaria lateriflora in two cups of water for five minutes.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for an additional fifteen minutes allowing flavors to meld.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container discarding solids.
Allow the liquid to cool completely before refrigerating it at least two hours or overnight.
Take one cup of the cooled scutellaria lateriflora decoction three times daily as needed for motion sickness relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme decoctions helps with motion sickness because of its unique ability to soothe the digestive system and calm the stomach. The aromatic properties of thyme, particularly its essential oils, have a gentle sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce feelings of nausea and dizziness. Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to ease stomach discomfort and cramping often associated with motion sickness, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating symptoms and promoting overall comfort during travel.
remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relieve nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by reducing inflammation in the stomach and digestive tract.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, another key component of thyme, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in thyme have been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness by soothing the stomach and gut.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Thymus are used in traditional medicine due to their ability to calm the stomach and alleviate nausea associated with motion sickness.
Barks: The barks of Thymus contain compounds that help to reduce symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 tablespoons of dried thymus vulgaris herb and add it to a clean glass jar.
Heat 1 cup of water in a saucepan until it reaches a gentle boil over medium heat for 5 minutes.
Pour the boiling water over the thymus vulgaris herb in the jar and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool down to room temperature before using it as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with motion sickness because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nausea and vomiting. The herbal remedy also has a calming effect on the stomach, which can help to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and lightheadedness. Additionally, rosemary's ability to improve blood circulation may help to reduce feelings of disorientation and discomfort associated with motion sickness. By consuming rosemary decoctions before or during travel, individuals can experience a reduction in motion sickness symptoms and enjoy their journey with greater comfort.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Rosemary's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles, which can ease the discomfort and nausea associated with motion sickness by reducing muscle spasms in the stomach.

Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to motion sickness, and also alleviate symptoms such as stomach cramps and diarrhea.
Vasodilatory property: Rosemary's vasodilatory properties can help dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing pressure on the inner ear, which can exacerbate motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have a calming effect on the digestive system, which helps to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Leaves: The leaves of the rosemary plant contain essential oils that help to relax muscles and calm nausea associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and place them in a heat-resistant glass container.
Pour 200 milliliters of boiling water over the rosmarinus officinalis leaves, allowing for a 10-minute steeping time.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into another heat-resistant container, discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Add 100 milliliters of cooled distilled water to the reserved liquid, stirring well to combine, and store in refrigerator.
Consume 2-3 tablespoons of the prepared decoction as needed up to three times daily for motion sickness relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage decoctions helps with motion sickness because of its unique ability to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in sage have anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate digestive discomfort, allowing the body to better absorb nutrients and regulate bowel movements. Additionally, sage's natural sedative effects can help to quiet an upset stomach, reducing symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and lightheadedness. By soothing the stomach and promoting a sense of calm, sage decoctions provide a natural remedy for alleviating motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Sage's antispasmodic compounds help to relax the muscles in the stomach and digestive tract, reducing the symptoms of nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which is often triggered by motion sickness and contributes to feelings of dizziness and disorientation.

Vasoconstrictive properties: Sage's vasoconstrictive compounds can help to constrict blood vessels in the face and head, reducing swelling and relieving pressure that can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are valued for their calming and sedative properties, which help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Rhyzomes: Sage rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat digestive disorders, including nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out one teaspoon of dried salvia officinalis leaves.
Boil one cup of water for five minutes in a medium-sized pot.
Combine the measured salvia officinalis leaves with boiling water.
Steep the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes before straining and cooling.
Drink one cup of the cooled decoction two times daily.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to motion sickness that are treated with herbal decoctions are nausea, vertigo, and seasickness. Ginger (Zingiber officinale) is a popular herb used to alleviate these symptoms due to its natural anti-inflammatory properties. Other herbs like peppermint (Mentha piperita), chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla), and lemon balm (Melissa officinalis) are also commonly used in decoctions to soothe the digestive system and calm the nervous system, providing relief from these unpleasant symptoms.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with motion sickness is ginger and peppermint. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate nausea and vomiting, while peppermint soothes the digestive system and reduces dizziness. Adding a small amount of lavender and chamomile can further calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress that often accompany motion sickness. This blend can be consumed as a tea or decoction to provide relief from motion sickness symptoms.
